Name:
Jori Xeric
(burgersdead)
Notes:
(Jul 30, 2001) (Bobby Wardell Sandimanie III is born in Atlanta, Georgia)
(2014) (Bobby starts making music)
(Nov 10, 2015) (Lone's first known song releases under the name 'Jori Xeric')
(Feb 23, 2016) (Lone starts releasing music under various names other than Jori Xeric)
(July 7, 2016) (Lone lies in an interview saying that he 'dropped my first song 3 months ago')
Streaming: There are only three known songs from this era, which we know from promotional tweets that Lone made in late 2015. Gorgeousrecords, a record label that promoted Lone's music in 2016, posted a screenshot of an interview on July 7, 2016 in which Lone lied saying 'I dropped my first song like 3 months ago.'

Name:
escort me out
(prod. greaf)
Notes: One of Destroy Lonely's oldest known songs. Promoted in a tweet from Lone on November 10, 2015 with the now-deleted SoundCloud link attached. Currently lost

Name:
bedroom trap shit dash dash
(prod. ???)
Notes: One of Destroy Lonely's oldest known songs, released only a day after the single in the entry above. Promoted in a tweet from Lone on November 11, 2015 with the now-deleted SoundCloud link attached. Currently lost

Name:
everybodygttnshot - father(jori mix hoe)
(prod. / ft. Destroy Lonely)
Notes:
Lost remix to Father's 'Everybody In The Club Gettin Shot' off of his 2015 album 'Who's Gonna Get Fucked First?', promoted in a tweet from Lone on November 24, 2015 with the now-deleted SoundCloud link attached. It's unknown if Lone did vocals for the track or only production. A day after release, Lone tagged Father on Twitter asking him to listen to the remix.

"@father can you check my remix of gahdamn Everybody in the club getting shot"

Name:
Tundra Boy Lonely
(Slim AU, racelonely, Glory Boi, Lil Gamo Belt, free lonely, Yung Gorgeous, Lil Citgo, Lil3x, Lil Fuck Yo Bitch Yung Gorgeous)
Notes:
(Feb 23, 2016) Lone starts releasing music under various names other than Jori Xeric)
(Early 2017) (Bobby starts releasing music under the 'Destroy Lonely' name)
Streaming: Bobby Wardell Sandimanie III was born on July 30, 2001. He has two younger half sisters and an older half brother. His father is the rapper I-20, who was part of Ludacris' record label Disturbing tha Peace. He was homeschooled starting in sixth grade then got enrolled back into public school in 9th and 10th grade, where he recalls abusing the drug Xanax. He cites his feeling that his substance abuse was destroying him as the origin of the "Destroy" part of his stage name. He spent his adolescent years in solitude which he cites as the origin of the "Lonely" part of his stage name.

Name:
NO STYLIST [V1]
(LORD, addicted to money, Overseas)
Notes:
(Mar 6, 2020) (Underworld is released)
(Mar 15, 2020) (NO STYLIST is announced as an 'EP')
(Apr 3, 2020) (It's stated that the project is 'almost done'
(May 1, 2020) (Lone states in an interview that it has become a full album now)
(Sep 30, 2020) (ᐸ/3 officially releases)
(Oct 31, 2020) (ᐸ/3² officially releases)
(Jan 31, 2021) (Lone announces that he signed to Playboi Carti's record label OPIUM & says the project is done but that he wants to put more songs on the project, leading to him reworking it)
Streaming: The first mention of NO STYLIST would date all the way back to a tweet from March 2020, where he teased the mixtape as a “mini project”: "after underworld imma give yall a mini project called no stylist nd imma give yall niggas swag nd let yall play wit it den disappear." Throughout 2020, Lone would drop various small EPs & continue teasing the album, before outright failing to drop it by years end. In January, he would get signed by Playboi Carti & begin changing his sound and artistic direction.

Streaming: The first mention of NO STYLIST would date all the way back to a tweet from March 2020, where he teased the mixtape as a “mini project”: "after underworld imma give yall a mini project called no stylist nd imma give yall niggas swag nd let yall play wit it den disappear." After continuous tweets mentioning the project, Lonely would finally tease a release date of October 1, 2021, in a now deleted tweet. Despite this, NO STYLIST failed to see a release on that date. After leaving the mixtape’s status entirely in the dark, it was released on August 12, 2022, eight months after the last mention with absolutely no promotion.

Name:
If Looks Could Kill
(Directors Cut)
Notes:
(Aug 12, 2022) (NO STYLIST officially releases)
(May 5, 2023) (if looks could kill officially releases)
Streaming:
Destroy Lonely first teased the project on September 15, 2022, in a now-deleted Twitter post, stating: "next album > “if looks could kill…” The rollout for the album would seem to officially start on April 25, 2023, when fans noticed a “billboard” in New York City with just the words “If Looks Could Kill” written in red on a mirror. The board would also show a QR code on the bottom right, leading fans to his website, revealing a countdown ending for May 5th, the supposed release for the album. On April 27, 2023, the tracklist of the album would somehow get leaked to fans and revealed 26 songs, 25 solo songs and 1 bonus track with Ken Carson.

When the album first released, it initially received lots of hate from fans. Many did not like it, as it was somewhat different from Lones previous music. However, when fall 2023 began to roll around and the albums deluxe (Directors Cut) dropped, many fans changed their opinions on the album and started to like it more. This is likely because the overall theme & vibe of the album was more halloween/fall, while the album dropped right before summertime. Nowadays, many consider this to be Lones second best album right behind NO STYLIST.

Name:
⭐ if looks could kill
(prod. Clayco, ​ssort & Carter Bryson)
Notes:
OG Filename: Destroy Lonely_ILCK_Main (Hi Res 24_88)
Initially previewed by Lone via Instagram Live on October 19, 2022, where he was seen wearing a Halloween mask, which fit the antagonist like aesthetic of the song. The track was recorded two days prior on October 17, 2022. The song was only teased one other time while Lonely was performing at a night club after a show on February 17, 2023. Prior to its official release, the snippet of the track gained a large amount of traction on TikTok, where over twenty-thousand videos were uploaded using the original snippet's audio.

Name:
LOVE LASTS FOREVER [V1]
(Love Last Forever)
Notes:
(May 5, 2023) (If Looks Could Kill officially releases)
(September 29, 2023) (Lone reveals the album's name, "Love Last Forever")
(Late February-Early March 2024) (Lone & Ayisha break up, Lone begins to rework the albums sound)
Streaming: After the release of his debut studio album 'If Looks Could Kill', Lone began working on his next studio album, the title of which was at the time unknown. An apparent tracklist for the album was posted to Lones (now ex) girlfriend on August 17th, 2023 with 13 tracks being visible and small portions of 7 song titles showing. The name of the album became public when Lone took to Tumblr to confirm the albums name, simply posting the words "Love Last Forever". The songs recorded during these mid 2023-early 2024 sessions were similar in sound to If Looks Could Kill, often more melodic. However, after Lone and his ex-girlfriend broke up, Lone began frequently working with producer 'Lil 88' to rework the sound of the album and drift away from the melodically evolved If Looks Could Kill sound. Some of the songs recorded during this era made their way to the 'LOVE LASTS FOREVER V2.5' deluxe. The cover for this era is fanmade.

Then on September 29, 2023, Destroy Lonely took to Tumblr confirming the album’s name, simply stating: "Love Last Forever." The song was previewed a couple more times throughout the following month of August and it released as track 11 on his highly anticipated album ”LOVE LASTS FOREVER”. Streaming: On March 23rd, 2025, Lone would take to twitter without any previous warning to announce to upcoming projects: A prequel to his 2020 mixtape 'Broken Hearts' and a collaboration with labelmate Ken Carson. Throughout the next few months, Lone would constantly preview many snippets, with almost all of them being loved and praised by fans. He also released some of these snippets to keep fans comfortable in the mean-time, releasing a 5 song EP on June 5th, 2025 featuring community grails like DARKSIDE & MONEY SHOWER,. Finally, On September 11th, after a period of little communication, Lone would announce that the album would drop on September 19th and release a final lead single, Jumanji.

(Collaboration With Ken Carson)
(00 & 01)
Notes:
(March 23, 2025) (Lone announces Broken Hearts 3 and a collab tape with Ken Carson)
(July 11, 2025) (Lone states at Beach Please that the tape is coming soon)
(September 19, 2025) (Destroy Lonely announces his next album will be called Drop Dead Gorgeous and that his next project will be the tape with Ken)
(Ongoing)
Streaming:
After the sudden halt on any word around the original version of Opium Twins from 2023, most fans assumed the album was scrapped, especially since at this point in time there was 0 news from insiders around the album. Ken & Lone continued to drop their respective albums (with features from eachother on each album) and focus on their own careers, leaving no hope for the project.

However, on a random sunday, after all the silence on the tape, with most fans believing it would never come, Lone took to twitter on March 23rd, 2025 to announce that he would be dropping two projects: Broken Hearts 3 and a collaboration with Ken Carson. The cover for this era is fanmade.

Name:
⭐ Ken Carson - the acronym
(with Destroy Lonely) (prod. Akachi, Sonickaboom & DJ Moon)
(Opium, Private Plane, Ostrich, O P I U M)
Notes: Snippet was posted on Ken Carson's new burner on Instagram (@xperimenting0_0), on Nov 15, 2025. According to Jamal, the song was in posession of sellers and could have been ran. Released on December 12, 2025 officially by Ken Carson.
